claim.content: The transport coefficient extracted from the low-temperature Fermi-liquid fit, $A=3.63\times10^{-2}\ \mu\Omega\mathrm{cm}/\mathrm{K}^{2}$ (from $\rho=\rho_{0}+AT^{2}$), together with the Sommerfeld coefficient $\gamma=93\ \mathrm{mJ}/(\mathrm{mol}\cdot\mathrm{K}^{2})$, give the Kadowaki–Woods ratio $A/\gamma^{2}\approx 0.45\times 10^{-5}\ \mu\Omega\mathrm{cm}\cdot\mathrm{K}^{2}/(\mathrm{mJ}^{2}\,\mathrm{mol}^{-2})$, a value that lies within the empirical range observed for heavy-fermion and strongly correlated materials and is therefore consistent with strong quasiparticle mass renormalization in this system.

conclusion.content: The transport coefficient extracted from the low-temperature Fermi-liquid fit, $A=3.63\times10^{-2}\ \mu\Omega\mathrm{cm}/\mathrm{K}^{2}$ (from $\rho=\rho_{0}+AT^{2}$), together with the Sommerfeld coefficient $\gamma=93\ \mathrm{mJ}/(\mathrm{mol}\cdot\mathrm{K}^{2})$, give the Kadowaki–Woods ratio $A/\gamma^{2}\approx 0.45\times 10^{-5}\ \mu\Omega\mathrm{cm}\cdot\mathrm{K}^{2}/(\mathrm{mJ}^{2}\,\mathrm{mol}^{-2})$, a value that lies within the empirical range observed for heavy-fermion and strongly correlated materials and is therefore consistent with strong quasiparticle mass renormalization in this system.

premises.content: Let $A$ be the transport coefficient in units $\mu\Omega\,$cm/K$^{2}$ extracted from $\rho=\rho_{0}+AT^{2}$ and let $\gamma$ be the Sommerfeld coefficient in mJ/(mol K$^{2}$); define the Kadowaki–Woods ratio $R_{KW}=A/\gamma^{2}$ with units $\mu\Omega\,$cm K$^{2}/(\mathrm{mJ}^{2}\ \mathrm{mol}^{-2})$. The proposition is that if for Ca$_{3}$Co$_{4}$O$_{9}$ the computed value $R_{KW}\approx0.45\times10^{-5}$ lies within the empirical range observed for heavy-fermion compounds, then this numerical agreement has often been interpreted as evidence for comparable quasiparticle effective-mass renormalization to that in conventional heavy-fermion materials, while noting that material-specific factors such as band degeneracy, carrier density, and dimensionality can modify the expected numerical value of $R_{KW}$.

steps.reasoning: Starting points taken as known from upstream conclusions: the Fermi-liquid transport coefficient $A$ determined from resistivity is $A=3.63\times 10^{-2}\ \mu\Omega\mathrm{cm}/\mathrm{K}^{2}$ (conclusion 1) and the Sommerfeld coefficient from specific heat is $\gamma=93\ \mathrm{mJ}/(\mathrm{mol}\cdot\mathrm{K}^{2})$ (conclusion 2); these two quantities are used to evaluate the Kadowaki–Woods ratio.

The Kadowaki–Woods ratio is defined here as the ratio $A/\gamma^{2}$ with $A$ in units of $\mu\Omega\mathrm{cm}/\mathrm{K}^{2}$ and $\gamma$ in units of $\mathrm{mJ}/(\mathrm{mol}\cdot\mathrm{K}^{2})$; substituting the experimentally determined values yields the empirical ratio $A/\gamma^{2}\approx 0.45\times 10^{-5}\ \mu\Omega\mathrm{cm}\cdot\mathrm{K}^{2}/(\mathrm{mJ}^{2}\,\mathrm{mol}^{-2})$ as reported by the authors.

The numerical value $A/\gamma^{2}\approx 0.45\times 10^{-5}\ \mu\Omega\mathrm{cm}\cdot\mathrm{K}^{2}/(\mathrm{mJ}^{2}\,\mathrm{mol}^{-2})$ lies within the range typically found for heavy-fermion and strongly correlated materials, and the authors therefore interpret this agreement with the Kadowaki–Woods empirical scaling as further evidence of substantial quasiparticle mass renormalization in Ca$_{3}$Co$_{4}$O$_{9}$.
[10]

motivating_questions.content: A quantitative test of whether Ca$_3$Co$_4$O$_9$ behaves like a strongly correlated metal requires checking established empirical scalings between transport and thermodynamic quantities. Before this study there was no direct experimental confirmation that the $A$ coefficient from $\rho=\rho_{0}+AT^{2}$ and the specific-heat coefficient $\gamma$ obey the Kadowaki–Woods scaling appropriate for heavy quasiparticles. Without this comparison, the claim that correlations produce heavy quasiparticles (and thus potentially large thermopower) remained qualitative. Demonstrating $A/\gamma^{2}$ within the heavy-fermion characteristic range provides a concrete, quantitative link between transport and thermodynamic renormalizations.

paper:867768477341974986.authors: P. Limelette | V. Hardy | P. Auban-Senzier | D. Jerome | D. Flahaut | S. Hebert | R. Fresard | Ch. Simon | J. Noudem | A. Maignan
paper:867768477341974986.en_title: Strongly correlated properties of the thermoelectric cobalt oxide Ca3Co4O9
paper:867768477341974986.zh_title: 热电钴氧化物Ca3Co4O9的强关联特性
